businessHours.title: Business Hours

partnerAiBudgets.description: Budget values are enforced across all child organizations. Dollar amounts are stored in cents internally.

partnerDefaults.maintenanceInvalid: Use a UTC window like <first>Sun 02:00-04:00</first> or <second>02:00-04:00</second>, or <always>24/7</always> for always-on. Leave empty to let orgs configure it.
partnerDefaults.maintenanceHelp: Time window (UTC) for automatic updates and reboots. Use <always>24/7</always> for always-on, or leave empty so orgs configure it individually.

partnerEventLogs.description: When enabled, all child organizations will ship event logs to the configured endpoint. Works with any Elasticsearch/OpenSearch-compatible <bulk>_bulk</bulk> store (Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Wazuh indexer, AWS OpenSearch Service). Leave disabled to let each organization configure individually.

agentVersionPins.title: Update version targets
agentVersionPins.description: Pin the agent and watchdog to a specific registered version, or leave on Latest promoted to track the globally promoted release. Agent and watchdog are independent.
agentVersionPins.partnerDescription: A pin here is the default for all your organizations; an organization can override it.
agentVersionPins.organizationDescription: An organization pin overrides the partner default.

billablesExport.title: Billables export
billablesExport.description: Billable time entries and parts as CSV (up to 366 days). Includes approval status.

Best regards,

partnerBranding.imageTooLarge: Image too large after encoding (max 400 KB). Try a smaller or simpler image.
partnerBranding.readError: Could not read image. Please try a different file.
partnerBranding.blobError: Blob URLs are temporary and cannot be saved. Upload the file instead.
partnerBranding.urlError: URL not supported. Use an https:// URL or upload a file.
partnerBranding.imageHelp: PNG, JPEG, or WebP · max 400 KB after encoding · resized to fit 256×256
partnerBranding.invalidSavedLogo: Saved logo data is invalid. Remove it or upload a new one.
partnerBranding.customCssPlaceholder: /* Custom CSS applied to all child organizations */
partnerBranding.customCssHelp: CSS overrides applied to the portal for all child organizations.

partnerSecurity.inactiveWarning: Allowlist configured but inactive — the API isn’t seeing real client IPs. Configure proxy trust (TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS + TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDRS) for it to take effect.
partnerSecurity.statusUnavailable: Couldn’t verify allowlist status. The server still enforces any saved allowlist — double-check your own IP is covered before saving.
partnerSecurity.allowlistPlaceholder: Enter one IP or CIDR range per line. Leave blank to let each org decide.
partnerSecurity.allowlistHelp: Leave blank to let each organization configure individually. Use CIDR notation for ranges. Requires a configured reverse proxy so the API can see real client IPs.

partnerNotifications.pushoverDescription: Inherited by any per-org Pushover channel that leaves the matching field blank.

partnerRemoteAccess.title: Remote-Tool Providers
partnerRemoteAccess.description: Configure third-party remote-desktop tools (RustDesk, TeamViewer, AnyDesk, etc.) to launch when users click <connect>Connect Desktop</connect> on a device. Each device needs the matching per-device identifier set in its custom fields under the configured key. Without a default provider, the built-in WebRTC desktop session is used.
partnerRemoteAccess.builtInDescription: The default. Connect Desktop opens an in-browser WebRTC session to the device. No third-party tool involved.
partnerRemoteAccess.empty: No additional remote-tool providers configured. Click <add>Add provider</add> to integrate RustDesk, ScreenConnect, TeamViewer, etc.
partnerRemoteAccess.templateHint: Use {id} for the per-device value pulled from custom fields, and {password} for the preset password. Examples: rustdesk://{id}?password={password} (custom protocol) — https://acme.screenconnect.com/Host#Access///{id}/Join (HTTPS, opens in a new tab).
partnerRemoteAccess.customFieldHelp: Key under device.custom_fields holding the per-device identifier (e.g. RustDesk ID).
partnerRemoteAccess.passwordPlaceholder: Leave blank if the tool prompts on connect
partnerRemoteAccess.passwordHelp: URL-reserved characters are percent-encoded automatically. Saved server-side in partner settings; never embedded in the web bundle.

errors.schemeRequired: URL template must start with a scheme followed by a colon (e.g. rustdesk:, https:)
errors.schemeBlocked: That URL scheme is not permitted — javascript:, data:, vbscript:, file:, about:, chrome:, jar:, blob:, view-source: and filesystem: are blocked.
errors.idRequired: URL template must include the {id} placeholder for the per-device value.

inboundEmail.changeAddressConfirm: Customers using your current address will still reach you. New replies will be sent from the new address. Change it?

inboundEmail.title: Inbound email
inboundEmail.description: Turn email addressed to your inbound address into tickets.
inboundEmail.domainNotConfigured: The platform inbound domain isn't configured yet. Contact your administrator.
inboundEmail.unknownDescription: What to do with mail whose sender isn't a portal user, a mapped customer domain, or a reply to an existing ticket.
inboundEmail.quarantineDescription: Held in the Tickets → Review queue for manual convert or dismiss.
inboundEmail.triageDescription: Auto-creates a ticket in the triage org above.
inboundEmail.dropDescription: No ticket, no review-queue entry, no autoresponse. An audit row is still recorded. Use this to keep unmapped spam out of triage and the review queue.
inboundEmail.dropUnverified: Drop mail that fails sender authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
inboundEmail.dropUnverifiedDescription: When off, unverified mail is quarantined for review. When on, it's dropped silently. This applies to <all>all</all> unverified senders — including mapped customers whose individual message fails the check.
inboundEmail.enableAutoresponse: Send an autoresponse acknowledging new email tickets
inboundEmail.subject: Subject
inboundEmail.defaultAcknowledgement: Leave blank to use the default acknowledgement.

alertSeverity.title: Default alert severity

agentUpdates.title: Agent update policy
agentUpdates.description: Automatic installs agent updates during the maintenance window below (or at any time when set to 24/7). Manual blocks automatic updates entirely — agents stay on their current version until you update them yourself.

maintenance.title: Maintenance window
maintenance.alwaysDescription: Agents may install updates at any time, subject to the update policy above.
maintenance.windowDescription: Agents install updates only inside this window. Times are evaluated in UTC. A window may span midnight (e.g. 22:00–02:00).

errors.invalidWindow: Enter a valid window — start and end times must differ.
errors.storedInvalid: Your saved maintenance window was invalid and is being ignored, so agents may currently update at any time. Choose a valid setting below and save to fix it.

orgEventLogSettings.title: Log Forwarding
orgEventLogSettings.description: Forward collected event logs to an external Elasticsearch- or OpenSearch-compatible store (Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Wazuh indexer, AWS OpenSearch Service) for long-term storage and analysis.
orgEventLogSettings.disabledDescription: Enable log forwarding above to configure connection settings.

enable.title: Enable log forwarding
enable.description: When enabled, event logs are forwarded to your log store on a recurring schedule.

approverDevicesSection.thisDeviceCanNowApproveRequests: This device can now approve requests
approverDevicesSection.theseDevicesCanConfirmHighRiskApprovalsP: These devices can confirm high-risk approvals (privileged access, AI actions) with a biometric. Your phone registers itself automatically when you sign in to the Breeze mobile app; you can also register this browser with Windows Hello or Touch ID. All of this is optional — approvals still work without it.
approverDevicesSection.noApproverDevicesRegisteredYetSignInToTh: No approver devices registered yet. Sign in to the Breeze mobile app on your phone and it appears here automatically, or register this browser below to approve with a biometric.
approverDevicesSection.optionalYourPhoneIsAlreadyAnApproverOnce: Optional — your phone is already an approver once you sign in to the mobile app. Register this browser too and you'll be prompted for Windows Hello, Touch ID, or your device's biometric.
approverDevicesSection.thisDeviceCanNoLongerApproveRequestsWith: This device can no longer approve requests with a biometric. You can re-register it at any time.

mtls.description: Configure mutual TLS certificate lifecycle for agent connections.

lifetime.title: Certificate lifetime
lifetime.label: Lifetime (days)
lifetime.description: How long each agent mTLS certificate remains valid before renewal is required. Shorter lifetimes improve security but increase renewal frequency. Default is 90 days.

expiredPolicy.title: Expired certificate policy
expiredPolicy.label: When a certificate expires
expiredPolicy.autoReissueDescription: The agent automatically receives a new certificate when the current one expires. This ensures uninterrupted connectivity.
expiredPolicy.quarantineDescription: The device is quarantined when its certificate expires. An administrator must manually approve the device before it can reconnect.
